commands.review_summary:
The PR adds optional external-content provenance to plugin tool hook types and propagates it through before_tool_call, trusted policy events, and embedded-run tool hook context.

PR surface: Source +150, Tests +82. Total +232 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source inspection: the PR seeds provenance before tool wrapping, while runToolLifecycle does not update that context after tool results. I did not run a live repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blocker is a bounded code/docs repair on the PR branch: keep the new API direction, but make provenance update when same-run tool results add external content and document the public hook field.

target.title: fix(tools): propagate external content provenance to policy hooks
target.body:
Summary:
- Adds structured external-content provenance to tool-call policy hook types.
- Propagates that provenance through HookContext, before_tool_call plugin events, trusted tool policy events, and tool hook context.
- Detects EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT markers from the active embedded run prompt/session context and attaches source information to the tool hook context.
- Preserves existing hook behavior when no external content is present.

Why:
- External content is already wrapped as untrusted prompt text.
- Before this change, the post-model tool-call policy boundary did not receive structured provenance showing whether external content was present.
- Policy plugins could see the tool name, params, tool kind/input kind, run/toolCallId, derived paths, and session context, but not whether the tool call followed browser, web_fetch, email, webhook, file-fetch, or other untrusted external context.

Proof:
- Added a before_tool_call regression showing plugin hooks receive externalContent provenance on both the event and context.
- Added a trusted tool policy regression showing trusted policies receive the same externalContent provenance on both the event and context.
- Added embedded-run propagation so tool wrappers built for the active run receive provenance derived from wrapped external-content markers in prompt/session context.

Validation:
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts

Both validation runs passed locally.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal
